711.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'If the time of prayer comes and you find only a sheepfold, pray there. If the time of prayer comes and you find only a camel pen, purify yourselves and pray there.'
711.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika waktu shalat tiba dan kalian hanya menemukan kandang kambing, shalatlah di sana. Jika waktu shalat tiba dan kalian hanya menemukan kandang unta, bersucilah dan shalatlah di sana.'

712.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uthman ibn Affan (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever builds a mosque for Allah, Allah will build for him something like it in Paradise.'
712.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Uthman ibn Affan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang membangun masjid karena Allah, Allah akan membangunkan untuknya sesuatu yang serupa di surga.'

713.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When one of you enters the mosque, let him pray two rak'ahs before sitting.'
713.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian masuk masjid, hendaklah ia shalat dua rakaat sebelum duduk.'

714.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Humaid or Abu Usayd al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When one of you enters the mosque, let him send blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ, then say: 'O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy.' And when he leaves, let him say: 'O Allah, I ask You from Your bounty.''
714.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Humaid or Abu Usayd al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian masuk masjid, hendaklah ia bershalawat kepada Nabi ﷺ, kemudian mengucapkan: 'Ya Allah, bukakanlah untukku pintu-pintu rahmat-Mu.' Dan apabila ia keluar, hendaklah ia mengucapkan: 'Ya Allah, aku memohon kepada-Mu dari karunia-Mu.''

715.iwh_en: It was narrated that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Is spitting in the mosque a sin?' He said: 'Yes, and its expiation is to bury it.'
715.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apakah meludah di masjid adalah dosa?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya, dan penebusnya adalah menguburnya.'

716.iwh_en: It was narrated that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When a servant prays, he is in private conversation with his Lord, or his Lord is between him and the qiblah. So when one of you spits, let him not spit in front of him, nor to his right, but to his left or beneath his foot.'
716.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketika seorang hamba shalat, dia sedang berbincang secara pribadi dengan Rabbnya, atau Rabbnya berada antara dirinya dan kiblat. Maka apabila salah seorang dari kalian meludah, janganlah meludah di depannya, juga tidak ke kanannya, tetapi ke kirinya atau di bawah kakinya.'

717.iwh_en: It was narrated that the Prophet ﷺ saw phlegm on the mosque wall while he was praying, and he reached out and scraped it off, then ordered it to be re-plastered. He said: 'When one of you is in prayer, let him not spit or clear his throat.'
717.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ melihat dahak di dinding masjid saat beliau sedang shalat, dan beliau menjulurkan tangannya dan mengeriknya, kemudian memerintahkan agar dilapisi kembali. Beliau bersabda: 'Ketika salah seorang dari kalian sedang shalat, janganlah ia meludah atau mengeluarkan dahak.'

718.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw phlegm on the wall of the mosque, took a pebble and scraped it off, then said: 'When one of you clears his throat, let him not do so in the direction of the qiblah, nor to the right, but to his left or beneath his foot.'
718.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melihat dahak di dinding masjid, mengambil batu kerikil dan mengeriknya, kemudian bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian mengeluarkan dahak, janganlah melakukannya ke arah kiblat, juga tidak ke kanan, tetapi ke kirinya atau di bawah kakinya.'

719.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ came to him while he was sleeping in the mosque and nudged him with his foot, saying: 'I see you sleeping here.' He said: 'O Prophet of Allah, sleep overcame me.' He said: 'How would it be for you if you were to be punished in this very place?'
719.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Nabi Allah ﷺ mendatanginya saat ia tidur di masjid dan menyentuhnya dengan kakinya, bersabda: 'Aku melihatmu tidur di sini.' Dia berkata: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, rasa kantuk mengalahkanku.' Beliau bersabda: 'Bagaimana jadinya jika kamu dihukum di tempat ini juga?'

720.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was found to have slept in the mosque. The Prophet ﷺ was told about this and said: 'What a fine young man — or what a fine man — he would be if he were to pray at night.'
720.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditemukan tidur di masjid. Nabi ﷺ diberitahu tentang ini dan bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik pemuda — atau sebaik-baik laki-laki — ia akan menjadi jika ia shalat di malam hari.'

721.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When you see someone buying or selling in the mosque, say: 'May Allah not make your trade profitable.' And when you see someone announcing a lost item, say: 'May Allah not return it to you, for mosques were not built for this.''
721.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila kalian melihat seseorang membeli atau menjual di masjid, katakanlah: 'Semoga Allah tidak memberikan keuntungan pada perdaganganmu.' Dan apabila kalian melihat seseorang mengumumkan barang hilang, katakanlah: 'Semoga Allah tidak mengembalikannya kepadamu, karena masjid tidak dibangun untuk ini.''

722.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man was passing through the mosque carrying arrows. The Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'Hold their tips.' He said: 'Yes.'
722.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki melewati masjid sambil membawa anak panah. Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Peganglah ujung-ujungnya.' Dia berkata: 'Ya.'

723.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ in his illness kept throwing his garment over his face. When he was distressed he uncovered it, and while in that state he said: 'May Allah curse the Jews and Christians for taking the graves of their prophets as places of worship.'
723.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ dalam sakitnya terus melemparkan pakaiannya ke atas mukanya. Ketika beliau merasa sesak, beliau membukanya, dan dalam keadaan itu beliau bersabda: 'Semoga Allah melaknat orang-orang Yahudi dan Nasrani karena mereka menjadikan kuburan nabi-nabi mereka sebagai tempat ibadah.'

724.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b ibn Ujrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When one of you has performed ablution and then goes out with the intention of prayer, let him not interlock his fingers, for he is already in a state of prayer.'
724.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b ibn Ujrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian telah berwudhu kemudian keluar dengan niat shalat, janganlah ia menyilangkan jari-jarinya, karena ia sudah dalam keadaan shalat.'

725.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b ibn Ujrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When you have performed ablution and set out for the mosque, do not interlock your fingers for you are already in prayer.'
725.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b ibn Ujrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila kamu telah berwudhu dan pergi ke masjid, janganlah menyilangkan jari-jarimu karena kamu sudah berada dalam keadaan shalat.'

726.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever performs ablution and then goes out intending prayer, he is in a state of prayer until he returns.'
726.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang berwudhu kemudian keluar dengan niat shalat, dia berada dalam keadaan shalat hingga dia kembali.'

727.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'The angels continue to send blessings upon a servant as long as he remains in his place of prayer in which he prayed, saying: 'O Allah, forgive him; O Allah, have mercy on him' — so long as he does not nullify his ablution.'
727.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Para malaikat terus bershalawat atas seorang hamba selama ia berada di tempat shalatnya di mana ia shalat, mengucapkan: 'Ya Allah, ampunilah dia; Ya Allah, rahmatilah dia' — selama dia tidak membatalkan wudhunya.'

728.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'The Hour will not come until people boast about their mosques.'
728.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kiamat tidak akan datang hingga orang-orang membanggakan masjid-masjid mereka.'

729.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Juhayfah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out to al-Abta' at midday and prayed Dhuhr — two rak'ahs — and Asr — two rak'ahs. In front of him was a spear (sutrah).'
729.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Juhayfah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ keluar ke al-Abta' pada tengah hari dan shalat Dzhuhur — dua rakaat — dan Ashar — dua rakaat. Di depannya terdapat tombak (sutrah).'

730.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to have a spear planted (in the ground) for him, and he would pray toward it.
730.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa memiliki tombak yang ditancapkan (ke tanah) untuknya, dan beliau shalat menghadap ke arahnya.

731.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'When one of you is praying, let him not allow anyone to pass in front of him. If they refuse, let him push them back, for they are only a devil.'
731.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila salah seorang dari kalian sedang shalat, janganlah ia membiarkan siapapun melintas di depannya. Jika mereka menolak, hendaklah ia mendorong mereka ke belakang, karena mereka hanyalah setan.'

732.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to pray toward a camel's saddle placed in front of him.
732.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa shalat menghadap ke arah pelana unta yang diletakkan di depannya.

733.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pray while she was present, sleeping crosswise in front of him like a funeral bier, until he had finished.
733.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa shalat sementara ia berbaring melintang di depannya seperti keranda, hingga beliau selesai.

734.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'What cuts off the prayer of a man — if there is nothing in front of him like the back of a saddle — is a donkey, a black dog, and a woman.' I asked: 'What is it about a black dog over a red or yellow one?' He said: 'I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ as you asked me, and he said: The black dog is a devil.'
734.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Yang memutus shalat seseorang — jika tidak ada sesuatu di depannya seperti bagian belakang pelana — adalah keledai, anjing hitam, dan perempuan.' Aku bertanya: 'Apa yang membedakan anjing hitam dari anjing merah atau kuning?' Dia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ sebagaimana kamu bertanya kepadaku, dan beliau bersabda: Anjing hitam adalah setan.'

735.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'A black dog is a devil.'
735.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Anjing hitam adalah setan.'

736.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he and al-Fadl came riding a donkey while the Prophet ﷺ was praying at Mina or Arafah. They passed in front of the row without the Prophet ﷺ objecting.
736.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia dan al-Fadl datang mengendarai keledai sementara Nabi ﷺ sedang shalat di Mina atau Arafah. Mereka melintas di depan barisan tanpa Nabi ﷺ keberatan.

737.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came while the Prophet ﷺ was preaching on Friday. He said: 'Standing forty (years, months, or days) is better for one of you than passing in front of a person who is praying.'
737.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang sementara Nabi ﷺ sedang berkhutbah pada hari Jumat. Beliau bersabda: 'Berdiri empat puluh (tahun, bulan, atau hari) lebih baik bagi seseorang dari kalian daripada melintas di depan orang yang sedang shalat.'

738.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Juhaym (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If the one who passes in front of a person who is praying knew what he incurs by doing so, he would prefer to stand forty than to pass in front of him.'
738.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Juhaym (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika orang yang melintas di depan orang yang sedang shalat mengetahui apa yang ia tanggung dengan melakukan itu, ia akan lebih memilih berdiri empat puluh daripada melintas di depannya.'

739.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'A prayer in this mosque of mine is like a thousand prayers in any other, except the Sacred Mosque.'
739.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat di masjidku ini seperti seribu shalat di masjid mana pun, kecuali Masjid al-Haram.'

740.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'A prayer in this mosque of mine is better than a thousand prayers anywhere else, except the Sacred Mosque.'
740.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat di masjidku ini lebih baik dari seribu shalat di tempat mana pun, kecuali Masjid al-Haram.'

741.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'A prayer in this mosque of mine is better than a thousand prayers anywhere else, except the Sacred Mosque.'
741.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat di masjidku ini lebih baik dari seribu shalat di tempat mana pun, kecuali Masjid al-Haram.'

742.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Do not undertake a journey except to three mosques: the Sacred Mosque, this mosque of mine, and the Mosque of al-Aqsa.'
742.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah mengadakan perjalanan kecuali ke tiga masjid: Masjid al-Haram, masjidku ini, dan Masjid al-Aqsa.'

743.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever walks in darkness of night to prayer, Allah will grant him a light on the Day of Resurrection.'
743.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang berjalan dalam kegelapan malam menuju shalat, Allah akan memberikan cahaya kepadanya pada Hari Kebangkitan.'

744.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Allah remains turned toward the servant as long as he does not turn away (during prayer). When he turns away, Allah turns away from him.'
744.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dharr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah terus menghadap hamba selama ia tidak berpaling (dalam shalat). Ketika ia berpaling, Allah pun berpaling darinya.'

745.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked: 'Which prayer is best?' He said: 'The prolonged standing.' He was asked: 'Which charity is best?' He said: 'The effort of one with little.' He was asked: 'Which migration is best?' He said: 'That you abandon what Allah has forbidden you.' He was asked: 'Which jihad is best?' He said: 'That you fight the polytheists with your wealth and your self.'
745.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ditanya: 'Shalat manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang berdirinya.' Ditanya: 'Sedekah manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau bersabda: 'Usaha orang yang memiliki sedikit.' Ditanya: 'Hijrah manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau bersabda: 'Bahwa kamu meninggalkan apa yang Allah haramkan bagimu.' Ditanya: 'Jihad manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau bersabda: 'Bahwa kamu memerangi orang-orang musyrik dengan harta dan dirimu.'

746.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever prays the two cool prayers (Fajr and Asr) will enter Paradise.'
746.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang shalat dua shalat yang sejuk (Subuh dan Ashar) akan masuk surga.'

747.iwh_en: It was narrated that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever prays Fajr is in the protection of Allah, so do not betray Allah regarding His neighbor. And whoever prays Asr is in the protection of Allah, so do not betray Allah regarding His neighbor.'
747.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang shalat Subuh berada dalam perlindungan Allah, maka janganlah kalian mengkhianati Allah dalam (hal) tetangganya. Dan barangsiapa yang shalat Ashar berada dalam perlindungan Allah, maka janganlah kalian mengkhianati Allah dalam (hal) tetangganya.'

748.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Arqam (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When the time of prayer comes and a man needs to relieve himself, let him first relieve himself.'
748.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Arqam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila waktu shalat tiba dan seorang laki-laki perlu buang air, hendaklah ia buang air terlebih dahulu.'

749.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade a man from praying while placing his hand on his hip (ikhtisab).
749.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang seseorang shalat sambil meletakkan tangannya di pinggangnya (ikhtisab).

750.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Barza (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ disliked sleeping before Isha and talking after it.
750.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Barza (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak menyukai tidur sebelum Isya dan berbicara setelahnya.

751.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was with Ali ibn Abi Talib when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent him, and he called out four things: that charity is not lawful for the family of Muhammad ﷺ; that no believer should be killed for an unbeliever; that whosoever has a covenant, his covenant extends to its term; and that the pagans should not remain in the Arabian Peninsula after this year.
751.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia bersama Ali ibn Abi Talib ketika Rasulullah ﷺ mengutusnya, dan ia mengumumkan empat hal: bahwa sedekah tidak halal bagi keluarga Muhammad ﷺ; bahwa tidak boleh seorang mukmin dibunuh karena orang kafir; bahwa barangsiapa yang memiliki perjanjian, perjanjiannya berlaku sampai masanya habis; dan bahwa orang-orang musyrik tidak boleh tetap berada di Jazirah Arab setelah tahun ini.

752.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sabrah ibn Ma'bad (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Teach your child to pray when he is seven years old, and discipline him for it when he is ten.'
752.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sabrah ibn Ma'bad (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ajarilah anakmu shalat ketika berusia tujuh tahun, dan hukumlah ia karenanya ketika berusia sepuluh tahun.'

753.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uqbah ibn Amir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'There are three times at which the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us to pray or to bury our dead: when the sun rises until it has fully risen; when it stands at midday until it has passed meridian; and when it sets until it has fully set.'
753.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Uqbah ibn Amir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ada tiga waktu di mana Rasulullah ﷺ melarang kami untuk shalat atau mengubur orang mati kami: ketika matahari terbit hingga ia terbit sepenuhnya; ketika ia tegak di tengah hari hingga ia telah melewati zenit; dan ketika ia terbenam hingga ia terbenam sepenuhnya.'

754.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'No prayer after Fajr prayer until the sun rises, and no prayer after Asr prayer until the sun sets.'
754.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada shalat setelah shalat Subuh hingga matahari terbit, dan tidak ada shalat setelah shalat Ashar hingga matahari terbenam.'

755.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ never failed to pray two rak'ahs after Asr in her presence.
755.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah melewatkan shalat dua rakaat setelah Ashar di hadapannya.

756.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ never abandoned the two rak'ahs after Asr.'
756.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ tidak pernah meninggalkan dua rakaat setelah Ashar.'

757.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ prayed Asr then entered while there were women from the Banu Haram of the Ansar, and prayed two rak'ahs. She sent a servant girl to stand beside him and say: 'Umm Salamah says: O Messenger of Allah, I see you are praying two rak'ahs after Asr, yet you forbade this.' He gestured to indicate that these were in place of the two rak'ahs he had missed after Dhuhr.
757.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ shalat Ashar kemudian masuk sementara ada perempuan-perempuan dari Banu Haram dari kaum Anshar, dan shalat dua rakaat. Dia mengirim seorang pelayan untuk berdiri di sampingnya dan berkata: 'Umm Salamah berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, aku melihat engkau sedang shalat dua rakaat setelah Ashar, namun engkau melarang hal ini.' Beliau memberi isyarat untuk menunjukkan bahwa ini sebagai pengganti dua rakaat yang terlewatkan setelah Dzhuhur.

758.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to pray two rak'ahs before Dhuhr, two after it, two after Maghrib, and two after Isha.
758.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa shalat dua rakaat sebelum Dzhuhur, dua setelahnya, dua setelah Maghrib, dan dua setelah Isya.

759.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm Habibah (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah  ﷺ said: 'Whoever prays twelve voluntary rak'ahs every day — other than the obligatory — will have a house built for him in Paradise.'
759.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm Habibah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang shalat dua belas rakaat sunnah setiap hari — selain yang wajib — akan dibangunkan sebuah rumah untuknya di surga.'

760.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ never abandoned four rak'ahs before Dhuhr and two rak'ahs before Fajr under any circumstances.'
760.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ tidak pernah meninggalkan empat rakaat sebelum Dzhuhur dan dua rakaat sebelum Subuh dalam keadaan apapun.'
